briefing.opening: Hello everyone. We will now begin the daily climate briefing for July 19, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Heatwaves and Wildfires Sweeping the Globe: Their Severe Impacts
sections.content: According to news reported today, the world is facing a severe crisis due to unprecedented heatwaves and wildfires. In Europe, heatwaves reaching 40 degrees Celsius, coupled with wildfires, are creating a 'worst-ever' situation [5, 6]. In the UK, 42% of the 2,700 summer deaths from the third heatwave have been analyzed as climate change-related [23]. Domestically, the 40-degree heatwave is causing people to lose sleep and become irritable [3]. In Arizona, USA, a teenage boy rescued an elderly person with dementia who was wandering in 39.4°C heat, highlighting the risks to vulnerable populations [32]. Wildfire smoke is now considered a sign that climate change is unavoidable [27]. Firefighters are being forced to make near-impossible choices, and the ripple effects are spreading in all directions, with even tariff threats against Canada being raised due to 'dirty' air from wildfires flowing into US cities [29, 30].

sections.title: ⚠️ Social Changes and Increased Vulnerability Caused by Climate Change
sections.content: Climate change is having widespread impacts on society and the environment globally, leading to growing calls for adaptation and warnings. In the Tampa Bay and Gulf regions of the United States, climate change is reshaping residents' living environments [14, 16]. There are also reports of changes in ocean currents [21]. Notably, the Lancet report warned that climate change exacerbates health crises in small island nations, expressing serious concerns for vulnerable regions [28]. UNESCO plans to list heritage sites threatened by climate change, alongside those threatened by war, on its 'in danger' list [19, 24], indicating that an alarm has been raised for cultural heritage protection as well. These changes are demanding adaptations in daily life, such as work attire shifting to 'Bermuda shorts' due to heatwaves and heavy rainfall [2].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic and International Climate Action and Renewable Energy Transition Efforts
sections.content: Active domestic and international movements and positive progress in response to the climate crisis are also being actively observed. Domestically, Kang Jung-il, Chairman of the Safety and Construction Committee of the Integrated Special City Council, emphasized the diligent promotion of climate change response and citizen safety projects [1]. The nation's first climate insurance, which compensates daily wages even for breaks due to heatwaves, has been introduced, strengthening the social safety net [4]. Public participation in carbon neutrality practices is also active, with university students in Seogwipo acting as 'carbon neutrality evangelists' and a carbon neutrality practice experience booth successfully operating in Dangjin [7, 8]. The government is accelerating the transition to renewable energy by reforming the offshore wind power purchase agreement and fixed-price contract systems [10]. International investments, such as a proposed $57 billion offshore wind project in Can Tho, Vietnam [11], and research activities, like a graduate student from Mokpo National University winning an excellent thesis award [9], are also active. Furthermore, new environmental performance indices are highlighting sustainability achievements [18], and educational efforts to teach children zero-waste habits and fight climate change are also continuing [26].

sections.title: 🌍 Scientific Exploration and New Approaches for Climate Crisis Response
sections.content: Discussions on scientific exploration and innovative approaches for climate crisis response are actively underway. US company Reflect Orbital has received approval for a plan to launch large mirror satellites into Earth's orbit by the end of this year to reflect sunlight, aiming to deploy 50,000 satellites by 2035 [12]. This is sparking debate alongside the potential possibilities of geoengineering. Meanwhile, a 37-year-long forest floor heating experiment observed the impact of climate change on soil, revealing that stored carbon began to leak as expected [22]. These scientific explorations and new endeavors provide a crucial foundation for understanding the interactions of complex Earth systems and seeking effective climate solutions.

briefing.closing: As we have seen in today's briefing, the world is facing serious threats from climate change, while simultaneously continuing various efforts to overcome them. Moving forward, continued attention and proactive responses to climate change will be crucial. This concludes the daily climate briefing for July 19, 2026.

articles.title: Controversy Surrounds 'Space Mirrors' Planned for Earth Orbit... Why?
articles.original_title: 기어이 지구 궤도 띄운다는 ‘우주 거울’ 두고 시끌시끌…왜?
articles.description: The U.S. Federal Communications Commission has approved Reflect Orbital's plan to launch 'large mirror satellites' into Earth orbit to reflect sunlight, illuminating dark ground at night with solar light for electricity production and agriculture, targeting 50,000 units by 2035. The scientific community opposes this, with the U.S. Astronomical Society stating it will 'ruin telescopes,' and concerns are raised about potential obstruction of pilots' and drivers' vision. An artist's rendering of the 'space mirror' satellite to be launched by U.S. company Reflect Orbital later this year. Provided by Reflect Orbital. Blue sea and white...

summary.phenomenon: U.S. company Reflect Orbital has received approval from the U.S. Federal Communications Commission to launch large mirror satellites into Earth orbit later this year to reflect sunlight, with a goal of deploying 50,000 satellites by 2035.
summary.cause: The purpose is to provide illumination to dark ground at night using solar light, and to utilize it for electricity production and agriculture.
summary.outlook: The scientific community is protesting, stating that these space mirror satellites could interfere with telescope observations and obstruct the vision of pilots and drivers.

President Lee stated on X today, 'Thoroughly investigate the cause of this fire and...'

summary.phenomenon: On the 18th, a fire broke out on the 6th floor of the Coupang 32 Logistics Center in Seoknam-dong, Seo-gu, Incheon, with smoke billowing upwards.
summary.outlook: President Lee Jae-myung emphasized the need to thoroughly investigate the cause of this fire and to review the overall safety management system of large logistics facilities to develop effective comprehensive measures.

articles.description: As the climate crisis fuels more intense blazes, pushing them to new parts of the world, those tackling them are forced to ration resources and decide which to fightCésar Alcaraz had only just become a firefighter in the late 1990s when he found himself ambushed by a fast-moving blaze. Barely able to breathe and with no more water left in his truck, he and his colleagues fled an inferno ravaging Spain’s Montgó mountain region, wishing their bosses had sent more support.But nearly three decades o

articles.title: Poultry sector growth plan risks UK national security, campaigners warn
articles.description: Government’s food security push is said to rely on animal feed imports with vulnerability to supply chain shocksThe government’s planned poultry sector growth plan is a risk to national security, campaigners have warned.Earlier this month, the environment secretary, Emma Reynolds, told the Groundswell agriculture festival that the key to improving food security was consuming more homegrown produce, and said this was why the government had set up the Farming and Food Partnership Board, whose memb

summary.phenomenon: Activists have warned that the UK government's poultry sector growth plan could pose a risk to national security. Environment Secretary Emma Reynolds stated earlier this month at the Groundswell agriculture festival that the key to improving food security is increasing the consumption of domestically produced goods.
summary.cause: This is because the government's food security initiatives rely on animal feed imports, which are vulnerable to supply chain disruptions.

articles.title: Arizona teen hailed for helping save woman with dementia wandering in 103F heat
articles.description: Fire crew in Gilbert to honor Royal Cothrun, 14, for helping elderly woman who became disoriented amid heatwaveAn Arizona fire department is formally honoring a 14-year-old boy who recently helped save an elderly woman with dementia after she wandered miles off course amid potentially lethal temperatures, saying the teenager demonstrated “heroism … quick thinking and compassion”.Royal Cothrun was riding his bicycle in June in the town of Gilbert when he encountered Theresa Morgan as she struggle

summary.phenomenon: In June, 14-year-old Royal Cothrun helped rescue elderly Theresa Morgan, who suffers from dementia, in Gilbert, Arizona. Morgan had wandered for miles amidst a potentially lethal heatwave of 103°F (approximately 39.4°C).
summary.cause: An elderly woman became disoriented and wandered in extreme temperatures during a heatwave.
summary.outlook: The Arizona Fire Department will formally honor Royal Cothrun for his heroic actions, quick thinking, and compassion.
